title: Living in Kirkwood, MO — Complete Guide
content:
Kirkwood is an inner-ring suburb ~20 minutes west of downtown STL. One of the best places for small-town charm in a central location. Population: 29,350. Median household income: $117,439. Median home sale: $583,500.

Downtown Kirkwood: 16 walkable blocks, 100+ specialty shops and restaurants. Historic Train Station (oldest planned commuter suburb west of the Mississippi). Saturday Farmers Market with 300+ vendors. Annual Wine Walk, Holiday Walk, Witches Walk. 150+ years of history.

Housing Types: Victorian (late 1800s-early 1900s), Greek Revival, Italianate, mid-century ranch/split-level, newly-built custom (modern farmhouse, French country, colonial, craftsman). Condos downtown $200Ks to $2M+ luxury.

Historic Neighborhoods (9 districts, 1850s-1950s):
- Jefferson-Argonne: Oldest, near downtown. Colonial/Tudor revival, bungalows. Among priciest.
- Sugar Creek: Mid-century modern ranches on larger lots west of Kirkwood Park.
- Savoy: Mid-century single-level near Kirkwood High School.
- Others: Meramec-Highlands, Barrett Brae, Central Place, Craig Woods, North Taylor, West Argonne.
- Osage Hills & Greenbriar: Mid-century/traditional near Greenbriar Hills Country Club.
- Woodlawn Terrace: Classic and updated homes, generous yards.
- South Kirkwood/Big Bend: More affordable, quiet streets, mature trees.

Schools: Kirkwood School District (A+ Niche rating). Kirkwood High School ranked #8 in MO (1,730 students). 6 elementary, 2 middle schools. Private: St. John Vianney (all-boys Catholic), Christ Community Lutheran, St. Gerard Majella, Villa Di Maria Montessori.

Parks: Kirkwood Park (92 acres, aquatic center, athletic fields, tennis). Greentree Park (Meramec River, boat ramp, annual Greentree Festival — 65 years). Emmenegger Nature Park (108 acres). Grant's Trail. Meramec River Greenway Trail.

Dining: Billy G's, PJ's Tavern, Kirkwood Ice & Fuel Co, Mike Duffy's, One 19 North Tapas, Sunset 44 Bistro, Station No.3, Napoli Kirkwood. Entertainment: Kirkwood Performing Arts Center (KPAC), The Magic House (children's museum).

Events: Route 66 Cars & Guitars Festival, Strawberry Festival (53rd annual), Greentree Festival (65-year tradition, 3-day event with parade).

Development: Aria Luxury Condos (42 units, $1-2M, across from KPAC). The James (152 luxury apartments, completed 2024). 300 N. Kirkwood mixed-use (60 apartments, approved Jan 2026).

Healthcare: Bethesda Gardens, St. Agnes Home, Aberdeen Heights senior living. Nearby hospital access.
